medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient is a poor historian, reported history based on OSH records. Patient underwent laparoscopic cholecystectomy for acute on chronic gangrenous cholecystitis. Discharged from OSH after a course complicated by persistent diarrhea and altered mental status. Treated empirically for C. diff with all cultures negative. Persistent RUQ abdominal pain since surgery, which varies in severity; denies fevers, chills, or malaise and has loose bowel movements daily.
medical_history.past_medical_history: C. difficile infection, CAD s/p stent, Atrial fibrillation/flutter on Coumadin, Congestive heart failure (EF 30%), Pulmonary HTN questioned, OSA, Diabetes mellitus on insulin (with retinopathy, neuropathy, and nephropathy), PVD, Cataract surgery, Retina surgery, BPH, CVA s/p L Below Knee Amputation.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vitals: Temp 97, BP 118/78, HR 62, RR 18; Patient is A&O, NAD. Abdomen is soft, mildly distended, tender to palpation diffusely but patient is distractible. Well healed surgical incisions.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Not specified; Cardiovascular: irregular heart rhythm, No M/G/R; Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally; Extremities: Right Lower Extremity warm with moderate stasis dermatitis, 1+ edema.
